title: Auto-run a lightweight viewer server when an execution session starts
description: The status-page viewer fails closed under file:// and today requires manually starting an http server. Ship a really simple view server that starts automatically when a session opens in a repo with execution set up, so the web page is always available. Likely mechanism: the SessionStart hook (rides the EXEC-043 per-feature hook plumbing) starting/reusing a tiny static server over the .excn JSON + viewer assets. Hard requirements: as lightweight and fast-running as possible — no framework, no build step, negligible startup cost, no orphaned processes (idempotent start, detect an already-running instance). Src-First.
acceptance_criteria: Opening a session in an execution repo makes the status page reachable in a browser with no manual step
Starting twice does not spawn a second server; no orphaned process after sessions end
Startup overhead is negligible and the server has zero runtime dependencies
Discovery record (port/pid/repo/started) written to the *_progress.json class and the URL surfaced into session context
Server binds localhost, GET-only, path-whitelisted to viewer assets + .excn JSON
fix: viewer_server hook feature: SessionStart hash-port auto-run, zero-dep GET-only whitelisted daemon with idle self-exit, discovery record, doctor liveness; off in template, on here. package-qa PASS incl. live smoke; code-standards PASS (two revise cycles: named constants x5, truthful comment x1).
notes: Filed 2026-06-10 (Brooks). Step 1 of the viewer-server track; validate here on the dogfood Instance before EXEC-047 generalizes it. Re-identified from EXEC-046 on 2026-06-10: the original id collided with a PRD-007 hook slice filed in a parallel session; sprint-4 close gate caught the lineage mismatch.
